concept: Agree / Reach Accord / Settle Together on One View
description: To agree: two people arriving at the same position after honest exchange. The verb of accord — stronger than politeness, weaker than promise ('seru', commit). Its natural frame follows discussion: 'shareo' then 'nawo'.
sound_symbolism: Grounded 'n', open 'a', and 'wo' — a shared settling, two weights coming to rest on the same point.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive with COM: 'mia nua thia nawo.' (I agree with you.) Of a group: 'sila nawo.' (The community agrees.) With content in the mena-frame: 'lo mia mena ha ruela welao nai meno nawo.' (We agree that this path is good.)

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Agreement is the destination of dialogue, and a language for peace needs the verb that names arrival — distinct from compliance, which Phi deliberately has no word for.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The village decision was made when the circle agreed — accord as the unit of governance older than any vote.
